input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

input.initialPrompt:
I have a .NET MAUI app with a ContentPage containing labels, buttons, and
a background color. Everything is hardcoded to light colors right now.
I want the app to automatically switch between light and dark mode based
on the device's system theme. Show me how to use AppThemeBinding to make
this page theme-aware in XAML and explain the C# alternative.

data.content:
## AppThemeBinding in .NET MAUI

`AppThemeBinding` lets you specify different values for `Light`, `Dark`, and (optionally) `Default` themes. The UI reacts automatically when the device theme changes.

### XAML â€” before (hardcoded)

```xml
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="White">
  <VerticalStackLayout>
    <Label Text="Welcome" TextColor="Black" />
    <Button Text="Go" BackgroundColor="#E0E0E0" TextColor="Black" />
  </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

### XAML â€” after (theme-aware)

```xml
<ContentPage BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=White, Dark=#1E1E1E}">
  <VerticalStackLayout>
    <Label Text="Welcome"
           TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=Black, Dark=White}" />
    <Button Text="Go"
            BackgroundColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=#E0E0E0, Dark=#333333}"
            TextColor="{AppThemeBinding Light=Black, Dark=White}" />
  </VerticalStackLayout>
</ContentPage>
```

No converters, no triggers â€” just inline `Light`/`Dark` values.

### C# equivalent (`SetAppTheme`)

The programmatic API is `SetAppThemeColor` for colors and `SetAppTheme<T>` for other types:

```csharp
// Colors
myLabel.SetAppThemeColor(Label.TextColorProperty, Colors.Black, Colors.White);
myButton.SetAppThemeColor(Button.BackgroundColorProperty,
    Color.FromArgb("#E0E0E0"), Color.FromArgb("#333333"));

// Non-color properties (e.g., an image source)
myImage.SetAppTheme<FileImageSource>(
    Image.SourceProperty,
    "sun_icon.png",
    "moon_icon.png");
```

### Reacting to runtime changes

The bindings update automatically, but if you need custom logic when the theme changes:

```csharp
Application.Current.RequestedThemeChanged += (s, e) =>
{
    // e.RequestedTheme is AppTheme.Light, Dark, or Unspecified
};
```

### Tip: use resource dictionaries for cleaner XAML

Define theme-aware colors once in `App.xaml`:

```xml
<Application.Resources>
    <Color x:Key="PageBg">{AppThemeBinding Light=White, Dark=#1E1E1E}</Color>
    <Color x:Key="TextPrimary">{AppThemeBinding Light=Black, Dark=White}</Color>
</Application.Resources>
```

Then reference them with `{StaticResource PageBg}` throughout your pages.

**Key points:**
- `AppThemeBinding` tracks the OS theme in real time â€” no manual wiring.
- `SetAppThemeColor` / `SetAppTheme<T>` are the C# equivalents.
- Extract repeated values into resource dictionaries to keep pages clean.
